design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate multi-screen non-scrolling levels with realistic physics-based jumping and climbing, solving spatial puzzles to progress while managing limited resources (ammunition, shield energy)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Realistic human movement constraints (limited jump height, fall damage, weakness) creates vulnerability; players must carefully plan navigation through screen-based environments with multiple altitude levels
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Amnesiac secret agent uncovering a conspiracy, using intelligence and resourcefulness to survive deadly situations and uncover alien infiltration
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately constrains player movement to realistic human physics rather than arcade conventions; emphasizes deliberate spatial puzzle-solving over combat; rotoscoped animation creates cinematic presentation that prioritizes visual authenticity over gameplay responsiveness

difficulty.checkpointing: Screen-by-screen basis; progression requires completing spatial navigation on each non-scrolling screen
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; realistic physics constraints are fixed. Resource management provides some agency through shield and ammunition management

progression.upgrade_system: Teleportation device acquired late in game enables access to otherwise unreachable areas; force field and pistol with unlimited ammo provided from start
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear level progression through seven levels; spatial puzzles gate advancement within each level
replayability_hooks: Skill-based execution of difficult platforming sequences encourages replays for optimization

world_building_techniques: Memory recovery mechanic integrates plot exposition into gameplay (regenerator device restores Conrad's memories)
Environmental context: jungle on Titan, city of New Washington, alien hideout
Dialogue and cutscenes establish conspiracy plot
Gladiatorial tournament (Death Tower) provides narrative context for player progression

notable_bosses: Corrupt police officers (thematic antagonists representing infiltration)

faction_indicators: Morphs: alien infiltrators disguised as humans
Human authorities: corrupted or controlled by Morph influence
danger_signals: Hidden underground Morph headquarters beneath civilian structures
environmental_storytelling: Jungle setting on alien planet Titan establishes displaced/colonized world
Nightclub concealing dangerous research facility suggests infiltration into civilian infrastructure
Vent system enabling espionage highlights vulnerability of secure spaces
